categories.label: AI & Cloud Infrastructure Investment
categories.description: Global data center buildouts, cloud capacity expansion, energy procurement deals (nuclear, renewables), and large-scale capital deployment for AI infrastructure across dozens of countries.

categories.label: OpenAI Partnership & Governance
categories.description: Microsoft's evolving relationship with OpenAI including funding rounds, board dynamics, the Altman firing and reinstatement saga, structural changes to OpenAI's corporate form, and talent flows between the two organizations.

categories.label: Cybersecurity Incidents & Response
categories.description: Cyberattacks on Microsoft systems (Russian state hackers, Chinese breaches), major service outages (CrowdStrike/Azure), congressional testimony on security lapses, and cybersecurity product launches.

categories.label: Antitrust & Regulatory Scrutiny
categories.description: Antitrust investigations and complaints across multiple jurisdictions (EU Teams bundling, CISPE cloud complaint, UK Inflection AI probe, FTC inquiries, Google complaints) and regulatory compliance actions.

categories.label: Gaming & Entertainment Strategy
categories.description: Xbox and Activision Blizzard integration, gaming layoffs, cross-platform game releases, Call of Duty subscription strategy, next-generation console planning, and gaming division restructuring.

categories.label: AI Products & Enterprise Partnerships
categories.description: Copilot features and rollouts, AI PC launches, autonomous agent development, and strategic enterprise partnerships (Vodafone, Sainsbury's, SAP, Cognizant, Palantir) integrating AI into customer workflows.

categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue and EPS figures, cloud revenue guidance revisions, dividend declarations, share buyback programs, and specific financial metrics versus analyst expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups mentioning MSFT, tech sector rotation, Magnificent Seven narratives, macro events (Fed rate decisions, global selloffs), and general AI investment sentiment pieces.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, and rating reaffirmations from firms including UBS, Wedbush, HSBC, BofA, and New Street Research.

dimensions.scale: 1=trivial mention with no connection to revenue, margins, or strategic position, 10=directly and substantially affects a major revenue stream, cost structure, or total addressable market
1=outcome or content fully expected and already reflected in consensus expectations, 10=outcome or revelation completely unanticipated by analysts and investors
1=impact limited to the next few trading days at most, 10=structural shift affecting the company's trajectory for years
1=neutral or purely factual tone with no positive or negative valence, 10=extremely strong positive or negative sentiment conveyed in the article
1=pure opinion, speculation, or rehashed narrative with no specific data, 10=dense with hard numbers, specific financial figures, or concrete operational details
1=ambiguous implications that could be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously and clearly good or bad for the company
1=affects only this company's specific operations, 5=sector-wide implications, 10=macro or market-wide event
1=no effect on competitive dynamics, 10=fundamentally reshapes competitive positioning or market share
1=no regulatory or legal dimension, 10=major regulatory action with potential to force structural business changes or impose large penalties
1=no leadership, governance, or strategic direction angle, 10=major leadership change, strategic pivot, or governance restructuring
1=transient story that resolves within a day and leaves no residue, 10=persistent story that remains in the news cycle and investor consciousness for weeks or months
1=reinforces the prevailing investment thesis and existing narrative, 10=fundamentally challenges or reframes the consensus view of the company
1=pure rehash or follow-up of previously reported information with no new details, 10=first report of genuinely new information

1=no financial results reported or results exactly match prior guidance and consensus, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst expectations as described in the article
1=no geopolitical dimension, 10=directly involves cross-border technology transfer tensions, export controls, or government-level strategic competition

kept.materiality: Ranges from trivial Azure Marketplace listings and minor partner announcements to multi-billion-dollar quarterly earnings, $60B buyback authorizations, and $100B Stargate project plans — wide spread across the corpus
kept.surprise: Altman's firing was a genuine shock; Russian hack escalation disclosures were unexpected; the CrowdStrike-linked global outage was unforeseeable; contrast with routine scheduled earnings dates, predictable data center investment announcements, and expected dividend declarations
kept.temporal_horizon: Service outages resolve in hours; quarterly earnings guide the next quarter; antitrust charges and nuclear power purchase agreements shape the business for years; AI infrastructure investments span decades of capacity
kept.sentiment_strength: Neutral tone in marketplace availability announcements and routine dividend declarations versus strongly negative security breach disclosures and strongly positive earnings beats with AI growth narratives
kept.information_density: Ranges from one-line flash headlines with no data to detailed earnings releases with specific EPS, revenue, margin figures, and segment breakdowns
kept.directional_clarity: Earnings beats are unambiguously positive; security breaches are clearly negative; antitrust probes and OpenAI governance reshuffling have genuinely ambiguous implications for Microsoft's position
kept.scope: Company-specific events like Xbox layoffs and Teams outages contrast sharply with sector-wide AI funding trends and macro articles about Fed rate decisions and Nasdaq corrections
kept.competitive_impact: Google filing formal EU complaints against Microsoft cloud practices, OpenAI pitching enterprise customers directly, and Mistral AI's rise as an alternative all carry competitive implications — versus routine partnership announcements with no competitive shift
kept.regulatory_risk: Extremely prominent and varied: EU Teams bundling charges, CISPE settlement, UK Inflection probe, FTC inquiry into OpenAI investment, South Africa cloud investigation, congressional security testimony — contrasted with many articles carrying zero regulatory dimension
kept.management_signal: Hiring DeepMind co-founder Suleyman to lead AI, the Altman firing and reinstatement saga, Microsoft leaving OpenAI's board, Brad Smith testifying before Congress, hiring GE's ex-CFO — versus routine product announcements with no leadership angle
kept.expected_duration: The CrowdStrike outage dominated one day then faded; the Russian hacker intrusion persisted across months of follow-up disclosures; EU antitrust proceedings generated articles spanning the entire corpus window
kept.narrative_shift: Altman's firing temporarily reframed the entire OpenAI dependency thesis; the security breach revelations challenged Microsoft's trusted-enterprise narrative; routine AI investment announcements merely reinforced the existing AI-growth story
kept.repeatedness: Many stories appear as first-break exclusives then spawn 5-10 follow-up articles with no new information (OpenAI funding round, Three Mile Island deal, CISPE settlement) — high variation between breaking reports and nth rehash
kept.financial_result_surprise: Three quarterly earnings cycles in the corpus with varying degrees of beat versus expectation; the Q1 intelligent cloud guidance cut was a specific negative revision; the $60B buyback and 10% dividend increase carried concrete financial content — while most articles report no financial results at all

dropped.actionability: Most MSFT articles cluster at extremes: earnings and buybacks are immediately actionable while partnership announcements and policy articles are background context — insufficient middle-range variation to be discriminating
dropped.controversy: Microsoft articles rarely present genuinely divisive investor debates; even contentious topics like antitrust and security breaches tend toward consensus negative rather than polarized disagreement among investors

added.geopolitical_sensitivity: A recurring and distinctive pattern in this corpus: the G42 UAE investment drew congressional scrutiny over technology transfer; Microsoft asked China-based staff to relocate amid US-China tensions; lawmakers probed chips-and-AI transfer risks; multiple articles covered AI export control debates — this dimension captures variation that none of the standard dimensions fully address
